archetype: Real-Time Cryogenic Hydrogen Embrittlement & Embankment Stability Predictor for Liquid Hydrogen Storage
description: A safety-critical distributed system that ingests real-time data from fiber-optic Brillouin scattering sensors and acoustic emission arrays to predict hydrogen-induced embrittlement in steel storage tanks before catastrophic failure. It correlates sub-millikelvin temperature gradients, pressure fluctuation harmonics, and local magnetic permeability shifts to forecast micro-fracture propagation in real-time. The system is deployed at large-scale hydrogen refueling hubs and green hydrogen production plants where current regulations require predictive maintenance rather than scheduled inspection.
rules: MUST process sensory data streams at sub-10ms intervals using IEEE 1451.5 compliant wireless sensor nodes to detect acoustic emissions associated with micro-crack formation.
MUST implement deterministic state transitions adhering to IEC 61508 SIL-3 safety integrity levels for any automated valve isolation logic.
MUST utilize finite element analysis (FEA) mesh updating algorithms that run on the edge to simulate stress distribution changes based on real-time thermal contraction coefficients.
MUST enforce strict data locality for cryptographic signing of safety logs using NIST SP 800-185 SHAKE128 to prevent tampering with forensic accident data.
MUST interface with legacy SCADA protocols (IEC 60870-5-104) while simultaneously ingesting high-frequency vibration data from piezoelectric transducers via proprietary binary framing.
anti_patterns: DO NOT rely on cloud-only batch processing for anomaly detection — latency in detecting a propagating crack in liquid hydrogen tanks (which can lead to BLEVEs) renders reactive cloud analysis fatal.
DO NOT use generic SQL transactional locking for sensor ingestion — the write throughput of high-frequency acoustic sensors will cause deadlocks, violating real-time safety windows.

DO NOT approximate material fatigue models with linear regression — hydrogen embrittlement is a highly non-linear, stochastic process requiring physics-informed neural networks or Monte Carlo simulations to be accurate.
key_technical_challenges: Synchronizing multi-modal sensor data (thermal, acoustic, magnetic, strain) sampled at different frequencies (1Hz to 1MHz) with nanosecond precision to correlate physical phenomena accurately.
Running complex, physics-based finite element simulations on edge devices with limited computational power without introducing latency that compromises the safety loop.
Maintaining system integrity and remote update capability in environments with extreme electromagnetic interference (EMI) and cryogenic temperatures that affect standard electronics.

recommended_stack.language: Rust — Provides memory safety guarantees and zero-cost abstractions essential for SIL-3 safety-critical embedded systems.
recommended_stack.framework: Zephyr RTOS — A real-time operating system designed for constrained, safety-critical devices with built-in support for IEC 61508 certification.
recommended_stack.database_engine: TimescaleDB with hypertables partitioned by time and sensor_id — Optimized for high-velocity time-series data with efficient compression for archival forensic analysis.
recommended_stack.cache: Redis with persistent AOF (Append Only File) and minimal TTL — To buffer high-throughput sensor bursts before ingestion while ensuring no data loss on restart.
recommended_stack.message_broker: MQTT with QoS 2 and TLS 1.3 — Standard for industrial IoT telemetry where guaranteed delivery and message ordering are non-negotiable for safety events.
special_services: Physics-Informed Neural Network (PINN) Inference Engine for micro-fracture prediction
Brillouin Scattering Signal Processing Module for strain mapping
